rubric_items.reasoning: All four known issues (image tag, serviceAccountName, memory format, missing pods RBAC) were corrected exactly as required, and the fix was verified end-to-end: the job reached Complete (1/1), the pod log shows a genuine Monte Carlo Pi computation completing all 1000 tasks, and exit code 0 was confirmed via kubectl wait/logs. This is a root-cause config fix (not a workaround) that would survive a restart or re-check.

The visible tail of the log shows a coherent, non-flailing sequence: monitor task progress, recognize the job is legitimately still running rather than stuck, then use `kubectl wait --for=condition=complete --timeout=300s` instead of repeated manual polling, followed by targeted log verification (grep for 'pi is'/'exit code'). The K8s activity (29 calls, only 3 mutations) is consistent with a lean diagnose-then-fix-then-verify path matching the 4 known issues. Slight deduction because the log head (initial diagnosis/fix-application phase) is truncated and not directly observable.

Commands used are targeted and appropriate: kubectl get/logs for diagnosis, kubectl wait with a bounded timeout for completion polling, kubectl logs with a label selector and grep for scoped verification, and (per the submission and expected mutation count) a scoped role patch and job re-apply for fixes. No broad or destructive commands beyond the job delete/recreate that the task itself calls for.

Only 3 mutating K8s calls were needed to fix all 4 identified issues (job spec corrections bundled into one delete+reapply, plus one RBAC role patch), with the remaining 26 calls being reads/waits for diagnosis and verification. This is a minimal, focused change with no collateral churn beyond what the task explicitly required.
